file_insights.test_coverage: Added comprehensive tests for dispatcher lifecycle and polling with assertions for process management, timeout behavior, and state transitions

patterns_discovered.description: Kill existing process via pkill pattern matching, start with nohup and background execution (&), capture logs via o>> and e>&1 redirection
Loop-based state polling with elapsed time tracking, fixed 200ms intervals, configurable timeout
Sequential state transitions (pending → running → done) with dynamic timeout allocation and early exit on timeout
Card state determined by directory location (.cards/STATE/ID.jobcard/), query via find_card_in helper
All external commands wrapped with 'do { ... } | complete' to handle non-zero exits gracefully

gotchas_discovered.description: Must pkill existing dispatcher before starting new one to avoid multiple instances competing for same cards_dir
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Added explicit pkill in start_dispatcher with do { } | complete wrapper

gotchas_discovered.description: Nushell has no native & redirect; must use ^nohup with o>> e>&1 for proper log capture
Current 50/50 split of total timeout between pending→running and running→done may be suboptimal if adapter has skewed latency profile
gotchas_discovered.impact: Could timeout prematurely on slow-to-complete states or waste time on fast states

gotchas_discovered.description: Fixed 200ms poll interval is arbitrary; no backoff strategy means constant CPU/FS pressure during long waits
gotchas_discovered.impact: May cause issues at scale or on slower filesystems

gotchas_discovered.description: If card moves to target state between loop iterations, poll may miss it depending on filesystem timing
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: 200ms interval makes this unlikely but not impossible; consider inotify-based approach

discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS - Dispatcher execution and polling logic fully implemented with 4 new functions providing clean abstractions. All 3 test cases pass: basic polling with timeout, multi-state completion, and process lifecycle management. Code integrates smoothly with existing test infrastructure.

recommendations.suggestion: Parameterize poll interval (currently hardcoded 200ms) to allow tuning per adapter/environment
Implement exponential backoff in poll_for_state to reduce filesystem load during long waits
Add instrumentation (start/end timestamps) to capture time-to-running and time-to-done metrics for performance monitoring
Make timeout allocation strategy configurable rather than hard 50/50 split to handle adapters with asymmetric latency
Document expected dispatcher behavior contract (e.g., guaranteed state transitions, atomicity guarantees) in script comments
